qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated queue execution permits unauthorized job processing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies an authorization bypass in QueueController::actionRun() where allowAnonymous=['run'] permits unauthenticated queue execution. This is unrelated to the advisory-described IDOR in Elements::parseRefs via GraphQL @parseRefs. The vulnerable component (QueueController vs Elements service), attack vector (HTTP endpoint vs GraphQL directive), and missing security check (authentication vs canView() authorization) are different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Zip slip path traversal in database restore permits arbitrary file overwrite
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies a path traversal vulnerability in DbController::actionRestore() via unchecked ZipArchive::extractTo(). This is unrelated to the advisory-described IDOR in Elements::parseRefs. The vulnerable component (DbController console command vs Elements service GraphQL endpoint), vulnerability class (path traversal vs IDOR/authorization bypass), attack vector (malicious ZIP file vs GraphQL reference tags), and required privilege level (local console access vs network access) are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes an IDOR vulnerability in craft\services\Elements::parseRefs where the GraphQL @parseRefs directive fails to perform canView() authorization checks when resolving reference tags like {user:1:email}, allowing unauthorized data access. The two qualifying findings identify entirely different vulnerabilities: (1) authorization bypass in queue execution via allowAnonymous=['run'], and (2) path traversal in database restore via unchecked ZipArchive::extractTo(). Neither involves the Elements::parseRefs method, GraphQL @parseRefs directive, or the missing canView() authorization check described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory identifies vulnerable component: craft\services\Elements::parseRefs and _getRefTokenReplacement method
Advisory describes root cause: Missing canView() authorization check when resolving GraphQL reference tags
Advisory attack vector: GraphQL queries using @parseRefs directive with reference tags like {user:1:email}
Finding 'unauthenticated-queue-execution': Authorization bypass in QueueController (src/controllers/QueueController.php:38,76-101) - different vulnerable component and missing check
Finding 'zip-slip-db-restore': Path traversal in DbController (src/console/controllers/DbController.php:340-361) - different vulnerability class, component, and attack vector
